report_name: Threat Actor 'JadePuffer' Uses Agentic AI to Autonomously Breach and Deploy Ransomware

threat_actors.name: JadePuffer
threat_actors.description: A threat actor who deployed a first-of-its-kind agentic AI to autonomously conduct a full-cycle ransomware attack, from initial breach via CVE-2025-3248 to data encryption.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The JadePuffer AI agent exploited CVE-2025-3248, a missing authentication vulnerability in the public-facing Langflow application, to gain initial access and remote code execution.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Collection
attack_patterns.description: The AI agent autonomously scanned the compromised system for secrets, API keys, and cloud credentials by searching for files with extensions like .env, .yaml, and .json.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The AI agent dumped the Langflow application's PostgreSQL database using pg_dump to harvest credentials and other secrets.

attack_patterns.name: Scheduled Task/Job: Cron
attack_patterns.description: The AI agent established persistence on the compromised server by deploying a cron job.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Using stolen credentials, the JadePuffer AI agent pivoted from the initial Langflow server to a production environment containing a MySQL database and an Alibaba Nacos service.

attack_patterns.name: Network Service Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The AI agent used tools like nmap and curl to scan the internal network and discover other services to pivot to, such as a production MySQL database and Nacos service.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The AI agent's final payload encrypted 1,342 service configuration items in Alibaba Nacos and created a ransom note in a MySQL database to extort the victim.

malware.name: JadePuffer AI Agent
malware.description: An agentic AI used by JadePuffer to autonomously execute a multi-stage ransomware attack. The agent exploited a vulnerability, performed reconnaissance, moved laterally, and deployed a dynamically generated ransomware payload.

malware.name: JadePuffer Dynamically Generated Ransomware
malware.description: A dynamically generated ransomware payload deployed by the JadePuffer AI Agent. It encrypted 1,342 service configuration items in Nacos and dropped a ransom note in a MySQL database.

tools.name: pg_dump
tools.description: Used by the JadePuffer AI agent to dump a PostgreSQL database to harvest secrets and credentials.

tools.name: nmap
tools.description: Used by the JadePuffer AI agent to scan the internal network and identify adjacent targets for lateral movement.

tools.name: curl
tools.description: Used by the JadePuffer AI agent to scan the internal network and identify adjacent targets.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2025-3248
vulnerabilities.description: A critical missing authentication vulnerability in Langflow that allows for unauthenticated remote code execution. It was used by the JadePuffer AI agent for initial access.

indicators.name: Langflow RCE Attempt via Run API
indicators.pattern:
title: Langflow RCE Attempt via Run API
id: 2a9b4c1d-8e7f-4a3b-9c1d-5e6f7a8b9c0d
status: experimental
description: Detects web requests attempting to exploit Langflow via the /api/v1/run/ endpoint, potentially indicating an RCE attempt similar to CVE-2025-3248.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/agentic-ai-used-to-conduct-ransomware-attack-via-langflow/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-03
tags:
  - attack.t1190
level: high
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    cs-uri-query|contains: '/api/v1/run/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects web requests targeting the Langflow /api/v1/run/ endpoint, which was used by the JadePuffer AI agent to gain initial access via CVE-2025-3248.

indicators.name: PostgreSQL Database Dump Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: PostgreSQL Database Dump Execution
id: 3b8c5d2e-9f6e-4b2a-8d0c-4e5f6a7b8c9d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of the 'pg_dump' utility, which can be used by attackers to exfiltrate database contents.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/agentic-ai-used-to-conduct-ransomware-attack-via-langflow/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-03
tags:
  - attack.t1213
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/pg_dump'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the `pg_dump` command, which the JadePuffer AI agent used to harvest secrets from a PostgreSQL database.

indicators.name: Cron Persistence File Modification
indicators.pattern:
title: Cron Persistence File Modification
id: 4c7d6e3f-0a5d-4c1b-7e9b-3d4e5f6a7b8c
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation or modification of files in cron directories, a common technique for establishing persistence on Linux systems.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/agentic-ai-used-to-conduct-ransomware-attack-via-langflow/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-03
tags:
  - attack.t1053.003
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|startswith:
      - '/var/spool/cron/'
      - '/etc/cron.d/'
      - '/etc/cron.hourly/'
      - '/etc/cron.daily/'
      - '/etc/cron.weekly/'
      - '/etc/cron.monthly/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects file creation or modification in cron-related directories, indicating a potential attempt to establish persistence, as performed by the JadePuffer AI agent.

relationships.source_name: Langflow RCE Attempt via Run API
